params.name: Statue of Gudea
params.description:
On Jul 24, 1887 AD, Statues were first discovered during excavations conducted by the French vice-consul called Ernest de Sarzec at Tello.
The Statue of Gudea is a stone statue made of volcanic rock, and its history can be traced back to the second millennium BC. Its solemn facial expression, with its hands folded in front of its chest, emanates a sense of piousness and calm as if it is praying to God.
